blurb: If you've ever walked into a liquor store expecting to grab a bottle and ended up spending 45 minutes wandering around in the best possible way, Tower Beer, Wine & Spirits is that place. This is not a corner shop. Reviewers describe it as "HUUGGGEEE" and they're not exaggerating — the selection spans everyday bottles to high-end wines stored in a dedicated glass cellar that people genuinely stop to admire. Cold craft beers, import options, a serious spirits section — the kind of depth that turns a quick errand into a discovery. If you're hunting down something specific for a dinner party or just trying to find a new go-to bottle, the staff knows their inventory and keeps things moving. Prices stay reasonable across the board, which reviewers consistently call out alongside the sheer variety. For Perimeter-area residents who've been settling for a grocery store wine aisle, Tower is worth the short drive down Piedmont. It's the kind of place locals end up telling people about.
hours: Mon–Sat 8:00 AM – 11:45 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
address: 2161 Piedmont Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30324, USA
scoring_notes: Strong ratings and review volume with a genuinely distinctive concept (liquor superstore with a glass wine cellar), though it sits just outside the core search areas near Piedmont Road.

blurb: Right off Lake Forrest Drive in Sandy Springs, Kai Medical Spa is the kind of place where people come in for one treatment and leave with a whole skincare routine figured out. The team handles everything from laser hair removal to facial treatments to skincare products — and the reviews make clear that the expertise behind it isn't just marketing language. One reviewer came back from spring break raving about their tan and their laser results in the same breath. Another described it as their standing "go-to" for all things skin, pointing specifically to the staff's knowledge and consistency across visits. That repeat-client energy shows up throughout the reviews, which matters. A medical spa is only as good as the people performing the treatments, and at Kai the team seems to take that seriously. Whether you're walking in for a one-time treatment or trying to build an actual skincare plan with professionals who can back it up, this is a solid spot. It's small, focused, and right in the neighborhood.
hours: Mon Closed, Tue 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Wed 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thu–Fri 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Sat 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, Sun Closed
address: 6095 Lake Forrest Dr NW #110, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
scoring_notes: Sandy Springs location fits the coverage area well, strong 4.7 rating with consistent reviewer themes around expertise and repeat visits — good fit for a community newsletter audience.

blurb: Permanent hair removal is one of those services where you really want to trust the person doing the work. At Smooth Electrolysis in Sandy Springs, Lynne has built exactly that kind of reputation. Every review points to the same things: she is patient, professional, experienced, and the environment she's created feels genuinely calm rather than clinical. That matters when you're talking about a treatment that takes multiple sessions and requires a practitioner who will actually take time with you. One reviewer put it plainly: "If you are truly serious about permanent hair removal, I highly recommend Lynne." That's not a throwaway line. Electrolysis is the only FDA-recognized method for permanent hair removal, and finding someone who does it well and makes the process comfortable is harder than it sounds. Lynne has a 5-star rating across all reviews, which for a specialty service like this tells you something real about how she operates. If this has been on your list for a while and you just haven't pulled the trigger, Smooth Electrolysis is a good reason to finally do it.
address: 6095 Lake Forrest Dr NW Ste 100, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
scoring_notes: Top score — Sandy Springs location is squarely in the coverage area, the service is distinctive and underrepresented in community newsletters, and the reviewer consistency around Lynne's expertise makes for a compelling, specific blurb.
